Hey everyone, how are you all doing? This is Anderson Grau, a Flamengo fan, and today we're going to discuss a topic that sparked some discussion on social media. Some people sided with Filipe Luís, some with São Paulo, and São Paulo said Flamengo's problem is physical. Filipe Luís said Bruno from Flamengo is technically gifted, and I'll give my two cents, which is worthless, but I'm going to say what I think Flamengo's problem is. I want to start by saying that I think both sides are right. Flamengo does have injured players, but it's impossible to blame it solely on injured players or physical condition in a game against Nublace. Flamengo has an obligation to win, whether playing in Alaska or in any environment, at home or away. Flamengo has an obligation to win against Nublace. Based on this principle, we discard the idea that it was solely due to physical issues. The issue is also tactical and technical, and I'll accept what Filipe Luís defends regarding free kicks. That's what Flamengo does... Flamengo isn't doing well. Technically, Flamengo isn't doing well tactically. If you have the ball all the time, you pass it around in front of their defense and you ca n't create a play, and that 's a tactical issue. Tactically, Flamengo isn't making the right runs, they aren't making the right infiltrations, they aren't making the right one-twos, they aren't making three-man overlaps to gain numerical superiority with the ball. These are problem situations. You don't see this frequently, I don't see it. I see passing and crossing the ball. That's what I see, at least in the last game it was very clear, as was the case with Corinthians, so that we could score a goal from a cross by a defender who was injured and went up to attack. So, it comes down to a point that I think is fundamental for Flamengo today: without figures like Arrascaeta and Everton Ribeiro, they don't create plays. These are the two main playmakers for Flamengo. Flamengo went onto the field with three defensive midfielders, so how do you play with three defensive midfielders? Thiago Maia isn't a man. Vidal isn't a creative player, and Gerson isn't a creative player either. That's why São Paulo plays with him more often. Gerson doesn't create, he's not the main player. Who creates? Everton Ribeiro or Arrascaeta. Without these two, Flamengo isn't prepared to create plays, and Flamengo needs individualism. Technically, Flamengo isn't doing well; the issue of confidence is that individual plays aren't working, and then they force the ball to Cebolinha, who isn't getting it right most of the time. That's why Flamengo doesn't create, doesn't create. Look, we had a shot on goal, Gabigol scored on a loose ball, Gerson chested it down, but the defender cleared it. Having creativity doesn't mean there's no game. So I think that would be Flamengo's biggest problem today, and then you see Flamengo's negotiations.
